U.S. and Interstate Highways are classified as state routes in New York; however, a letter ("U" or "I", respectively) is suffixed to the number of the route. As a result, there is apparent duplication between U.S. Routes, Interstate Highways and state routes.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Highway system

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| Standard route shields in New York | |
| Highway names | |
| [Interstates](/wiki/Interstate_Highway_System "Interstate Highway System") | Interstate X (I-X) |
| [US Highways](/wiki/United_States_Numbered_Highway_System "United States Numbered Highway System") | U.S. Route X (US X) |
| [State](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") | New York State Route X (NY X) |
| System links | |
| * **New York Highways**  * [Interstate](/wiki/List_of_Interstate_Highways_in_New_York "List of Interstate Highways in New York") * [US](/wiki/List_of_U.S._Routes_in_New_York "List of U.S. Routes in New York") * [State](/wiki/List_of_state_routes_in_New_York "List of state routes in New York") * [Reference](/wiki/List_of_reference_routes_in_New_York "List of reference routes in New York") * [Parkways](/wiki/Parkways_in_New_York "Parkways in New York") | |

The [New York State Department of Transportation](/wiki/New_York_State_Department_of_Transportation "New York State Department of Transportation") (NYSDOT) is responsible for the establishment and classification of a [state highway](/wiki/State_highway "State highway") network which includes [Interstate Highways](/wiki/Interstate_Highway_System "Interstate Highway System"), [U.S. Highways](/wiki/United_States_Numbered_Highways "United States Numbered Highways"), and [state routes](/wiki/State_route "State route"). U.S. and Interstate Highways are classified as state routes in New York; however, a letter ("U" or "I", respectively) is suffixed to the number of the route. As a result, there is apparent duplication between U.S. Routes, Interstate Highways and state routes.

The New York state highway system is supplemented by the state's county route system, which comprises a series of highways numbered and maintained by the individual county highway departments. While neighboring [New Jersey](/wiki/County_routes_in_New_Jersey "County routes in New Jersey") employs a statewide numbering system, no such system exists in New York. Instead, each county numbers its highways independently of other counties. As a result, county routes typically change numbers when they cross county lines.

In some cases, the state highway and county highway systems overlap. More specifically, some portions of U.S. Routes and state routes in New York are signed as U.S. Routes or state routes but are maintained by the county that the route lies within. These county-maintained segments also carry a county route designation that may or may not be posted alongside the U.S. or state designation, depending on the signing practices of the county. Some state routes, such as [New York State Route 148](/wiki/New_York_State_Route_148 "New York State Route 148") in [Niagara County](/wiki/Niagara_County,_New_York "Niagara County, New York"), are entirely county-maintained.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Numbered_highways_in_New_York&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

See also: [New York State Department of Transportation](/wiki/New_York_State_Department_of_Transportation "New York State Department of Transportation")

## Lists of routes

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | [Interstate Highways](/wiki/List_of_Interstate_Highways_in_New_York "List of Interstate Highways in New York"): A list of Interstate Highways within New York. |
|  | [U.S. Routes](/wiki/List_of_U.S._Routes_in_New_York "List of U.S. Routes in New York"): A list of U.S. Highways within New York. |
|  | [State routes](/wiki/List_of_state_routes_in_New_York "List of state routes in New York"): A list of all state routes, past and present, within New York. A list containing just [state routes that no longer exist](/wiki/List_of_former_state_routes_in_New_York "List of former state routes in New York") also exists. |
|  | [Reference routes](/wiki/List_of_reference_routes_in_New_York "List of reference routes in New York"): A list of all of New York's reference routes—all state-maintained highways that are not part of a state route. |
|  | [County routes](/wiki/County_routes_in_New_York "County routes in New York"): An article on county routes in New York. Contains links to by-county route lists..... |
|  | [Parkways](/wiki/Parkways_in_New_York "Parkways in New York") |

## Thruway

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Numbered_highways_in_New_York&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Thruway")]

Main article: [New York State Thruway](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway "New York State Thruway")

Highways maintained by the [New York State Thruway Authority](/wiki/New_York_State_Thruway_Authority "New York State Thruway Authority") lack any signed numbers apart from Interstate Highway designations. While the vast majority of the Thruway system is part of the [Interstate Highway System](/wiki/Interstate_Highway_System "Interstate Highway System"), some sections, such as the Berkshire Connector between [Interstate 87](/wiki/Interstate_87_(New_York) "Interstate 87 (New York)") and [Interstate 90](/wiki/Interstate_90_(New_York) "Interstate 90 (New York)"), are unsigned [reference routes](/wiki/Reference_route_(New_York) "Reference route (New York)"). There are no standard NYSDOT [reference markers](/wiki/Reference_marker_(New_York) "Reference marker (New York)") on any of the highways; instead, the Thruway Authority posts their own mileposts, which (aside from the mainline) bear special Thruway codes.
